description: Advanced senior-level chemistry covering quantum mechanics, bonding, organic chemistry, thermochemistry, kinetics, equilibrium, electrochemistry, and nuclear chemistry. Designed to prepare students for university-level chemistry and AP/IB courses.

topics.name: Unit 1: Quantum Mechanics & Atomic Theory
topics.description: Historical development of atomic theory, quantum numbers, electron configurations, atomic orbitals, photoelectric effect, and line spectra.

topics.name: Unit 2: Advanced Bonding & VSEPR Theory
topics.description: Lewis structures, formal charge, resonance, VSEPR theory, molecular geometry, bond angles, and polarity.

topics.name: Unit 3: Hybridization & Intermolecular Forces
topics.description: sp, sp², sp³ hybridization, sigma and pi bonds, intermolecular forces (London, dipole-dipole, hydrogen bonding), and physical property predictions.

topics.name: Unit 4: Hydrocarbons & Nomenclature
topics.description: Alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, cycloalkanes, IUPAC naming, structural isomers, and physical properties.

topics.name: Unit 5: Functional Groups & Properties
topics.description: Alcohols, ethers, aldehydes, ketones, carboxylic acids, esters, amines, amides, and their physical/chemical properties.

topics.name: Unit 6: Organic Reactions & Polymers
topics.description: Substitution, addition, elimination, oxidation, reduction, polymerization, and reaction mechanisms.

topics.name: Unit 7: Thermochemistry & Enthalpy
topics.description: Endothermic and exothermic reactions, calorimetry, Hess's law, standard enthalpy of formation, and bond energies.

topics.name: Unit 8: Chemical Kinetics
topics.description: Reaction rates, rate laws, half-life, activation energy, Arrhenius equation, and reaction mechanisms.

topics.name: Unit 9: Chemical Equilibrium
topics.description: Dynamic equilibrium, equilibrium constant (Kc, Kp), Le Chatelier's principle, and ICE table calculations.

topics.name: Unit 10: Solubility Equilibrium (Ksp)
topics.description: Solubility product constant, common ion effect, precipitation reactions, and Ksp calculations.

topics.name: Unit 11: Acid-Base Equilibrium & Buffers
topics.description: Strong and weak acids/bases, pH, pOH, Ka, Kb, buffer solutions, titration curves, and Henderson-Hasselbalch equation.

topics.name: Unit 12: Electrochemistry & Redox Cells
topics.description: Redox reactions, balancing half-reactions, galvanic cells, electrolytic cells, standard reduction potentials, and Nernst equation.
